# **UBL-TR Invoice Canonical Schema Report**

## **1\. Ecosystem Architecture and the Imperative for Normalization**

The architectural foundation of the Turkish trade finance ecosystem represents a sophisticated intersection of highly regulated financial mechanisms and advanced digital transformation initiatives. Rooted deeply in the legislative frameworks established by the 6361 Numbered Financial Leasing, Factoring and Financing Companies Law enacted in 2012, the operational environment is governed by stringent oversight from the Banking Regulation and Supervision Agency (BDDK).1 Following the consolidation of the sector into the Finansal Kurumlar Birliği in 2013, the industry formalized a rigorous operational standard that mandates precise choreography between commercial entities, financial institutions, and governmental digital infrastructure.1  
Within this paradigm, business-to-government (B2G) and business-to-business (B2B) electronic invoicing is strictly mandatory for enterprises surpassing specific turnover thresholds, utilizing the centralized infrastructure orchestrated by the Gelir İdaresi Başkanlığı (GİB).1 The foundational commercial document within this network is the electronic invoice (e-Fatura), which strictly adheres to the Universal Business Language (UBL-TR 1.2) XML schema.1 This schema is a highly localized, heavily customized adaptation of the global OASIS (Organization for the Advancement of Structured Information Standards) standard, engineered specifically to provide a royalty-free, open library of standardized electronic business documents designed to facilitate seamless, machine-readable data exchange without the necessity of manual rekeying.1  
The commercial finance landscape bifurcates into two fundamentally distinct streams: Conventional Factoring, relying on the legal mechanism of assignment of receivables (alacak devri), and Participation Banking (Katılım Bankası) mechanisms, specifically Islamic trade finance executed via Murabaha contracts.1 In conventional factoring, a commercial enterprise (Satıcı) supplies goods, generates an e-Fatura and an electronic despatch advice (e-İrsaliye), and seeks immediate liquidity by presenting these documents to a financial institution (Faktor) alongside an Alacak Bildirim Formu (AB-NTR).1 The Faktor advances capital based on the mathematical constraints defined in a Limit Onay Bildirimi (LOB).1 Conversely, the strict domain of Islamic Participation Banking requires the institution to verifiably acquire actual legal title to the underlying commercial goods before executing a subsequent resale to the corporate recipient (Alıcı) at a transparent markup, utilizing a Murabaha Satış Sözleşmesi.1  
Both workflows suffer from profound operational friction when human operators—Client Administrators—are tasked with manually reviewing complex, multi-layered document packs.1 The operational requirement to integrate highly structured, machine-readable XML data arrays with fundamentally unstructured physical documents creates a massive administrative burden, heavily susceptible to data entry mismatch and transposition discrepancies.1 The sheer volume of complex documentation routinely stretches administrative resources to their absolute limits, necessitating the deployment of automated, mathematically rigid consistency check mechanisms.1  
To operationalize programmatic verification, the deeply nested, hierarchical UBL-TR 1.2 XML arrays must be systematically extracted and flattened into a relational, highly optimized canonical JSON schema.1 This structural translation entirely bypasses the visual representations (the HTML or PDF rendered stylesheets) of the e-Fatura, neutralizing human cognitive bottlenecks and enabling high-velocity deterministic rule engines to identify any discrepancy or inconsistency within the commercial narrative.1 The canonical JSON acts as an immutable digital reflection of the commercial truth, facilitating exhaustive mathematical cross-examination (tutar tutarlılığı) and definitive corporate identity verification (parti kimliği) entirely within a closed programmatic loop.

## **2\. UBL-TR 1.2 Namespace Resolution and DOM Traversal Mechanics**

The creation of a robust canonical JSON schema relies entirely on the precision of the initial Document Object Model (DOM) traversal. The UBL-TR 1.2 architecture employs a complex matrix of XML namespaces to segregate data components into logical domains.9 To successfully parse the e-Fatura document and extract the precise required string values, the ingestion engine must explicitly declare, map, and resolve these namespaces prior to executing any XPath query. Failure to accurately map the namespace registry inevitably results in traversal failures and silent data omission, generating a false discrepancy during subsequent consistency verification.  
The core namespace definitions required for programmatic XPath evaluation within the Turkish e-Fatura ecosystem are defined as follows:

* The Default Namespace (xmlns): Maps to the core document type, universally defined as urn:oasis:names:specification:ubl:schema:xsd:Invoice-2.9  
* Common Aggregate Components (cac): Maps to urn:oasis:names:specification:ubl:schema:xsd:CommonAggregateComponents-2. This namespace encapsulates complex business objects that contain nested child nodes, such as parties, lines, and monetary totals.9  
* Common Basic Components (cbc): Maps to urn:oasis:names:specification:ubl:schema:xsd:CommonBasicComponents-2. This namespace defines the atomic, indivisible data elements containing the actual string, numeric, or boolean values, such as identifiers, dates, and names.9  
* Common Extension Components (ext): Maps to urn:oasis:names:specification:ubl:schema:xsd:CommonExtensionComponents-2. This namespace houses the localized Turkish customizations and, most importantly, the cryptographic signature blocks mandated by the GİB.9  
* Common Signature Components (sig): Maps to urn:oasis:names:specification:ubl:schema:xsd:CommonSignatureComponents-2, utilized to navigate the digital signature envelopes ensuring the file has not been altered post-generation.9

When a programmatic parser evaluates an e-Fatura, it utilizes these prefixes to navigate the rigid hierarchical tree. For example, to identify the registered corporate title of the commercial seller, the parser cannot simply search for a \<Name\> tag. It must strictly traverse through the cac:AccountingSupplierParty aggregate, into the nested cac:Party node, into the cac:PartyName aggregate, and finally terminate at the basic component cbc:Name.1 This absolute structural unyieldingness allows consistency check algorithms to perform verifications with near-zero error margins, transforming the validation process from a probabilistic reading exercise into a deterministic mathematical computation.1  
The subsequent normalization process translates this deep nesting into a flat, instantly accessible JSON object, radically decreasing the computational complexity required for downstream processing engines to execute mathematical reconciliations.

## **3\. Exhaustive XPath to Canonical JSON Field Mapping**

The systematic generation of the canonical JSON schema requires an exhaustive, node-by-node mapping of every critical element within the UBL-TR 1.2 specification.15 This mapping translates the hierarchical XML syntax into flat, relational JSON keys, enforcing strict data typing and structural predictability. The mapping architecture is divided into foundational blocks representing the metadata, the legal entities, the physical shipment references, the financial settlement instructions, the taxation models, and the granular commercial line items.  
The following table provides the definitive mapping dictionary required to normalize a Turkish e-Fatura into the canonical JSON structure, detailing the exact XPath, the targeted canonical field, the mandated data type, and the extensive business rationale governing its extraction.

| Exact UBL-TR 1.2 XPath | Canonical Field | Type | Required/Optional | Description / Business Logic Notes |
| :---- | :---- | :---- | :---- | :---- |
| /Invoice/cbc:UBLVersionID | metadata.ubl\_version | String | Required | Validates the foundational OASIS structure. Must explicitly declare the string "2.1" to be recognized by the parsing engine.10 |
| /Invoice/cbc:CustomizationID | metadata.customization\_id | String | Required | Establishes the regional Turkish localization schema, strictly defined as "TR1.2" for GİB compliance.10 |
| /Invoice/cbc:ProfileID | metadata.profile\_id | String | Required | Defines the overarching business scenario, such as "TEMELFATURA" (Basic Invoice) or "TICARIFATURA" (Commercial Invoice requiring explicit acceptance/rejection).10 |
| /Invoice/cbc:ID | document.invoice\_id | String | Required | The deterministic 16-character alphanumeric identifier designated by the GİB (e.g., GIB2026000000011). Serves as the primary unique key for cross-referencing against the physical AB-NTR.1 |
| /Invoice/cbc:UUID | document.uuid | String | Required | The universally unique cryptographic hash generated during document creation. Utilized to establish an immutable internal index preventing duplicate invoice representations.1 |
| /Invoice/cbc:IssueDate | document.issue\_date | String | Required | The foundational temporal marker of commercial realization. The chronological sequence consistency check relies completely on this node to ensure physical despatch logically precedes invoicing.1 |
| /Invoice/cbc:IssueTime | document.issue\_time | String | Optional | Granular temporal stamp detailing the exact hour, minute, and second of generation. Merged programmatically into issue\_date during normalization. |
| /Invoice/cbc:InvoiceTypeCode | document.type\_code | String | Required | Delineates the document's nature from the UN/ECE 1001 Subset list (e.g., 380 for Commercial Invoices, SATIS for sales, IADE for returns).12 |
| /Invoice/cbc:DocumentCurrencyCode | document.currency\_code | String | Required | Defines the overarching commercial currency. Must strictly adhere to the 3-letter ISO 4217 Alpha-3 standard (e.g., TRY, EUR, USD).1 |
| /Invoice/cac:InvoicePeriod/cbc:StartDate | document.period\_start | String | Optional | Indicates the beginning of the commercial delivery or invoicing period, facilitating long-term contract reconciliation.14 |
| /Invoice/cac:InvoicePeriod/cbc:EndDate | document.period\_end | String | Optional | Indicates the conclusion of the specific commercial delivery or service timeframe.14 |
| /Invoice/cac:AccountingSupplierParty/cac:Party/cac:PartyIdentification/cbc:ID | supplier.vkn | String | Conditional | Extracts the 10-digit Tax Identification Number representing the exact corporate identity (parti kimliği) of the selling entity. Essential for cross-referencing against the Tüzel Kişi Müşteri Bilgi Formu.1 |
| /Invoice/cac:AccountingSupplierParty/cac:Party/cac:PartyIdentification/cbc:ID | supplier.tckn | String | Conditional | Extracts the 11-digit Turkish Citizen Identification Number if the commercial supplier operates as a legally registered sole proprietorship.1 |
| /Invoice/cac:AccountingSupplierParty/cac:Party/cac:PartyName/cbc:Name | supplier.name | String | Required | Captures the full registered corporate title of the commercial seller, allowing NLP comparison against the AB-NTR text.1 |
| /Invoice/cac:AccountingSupplierParty/cac:Party/cac:Contact/cbc:Telephone | supplier.contact.phone | String | Optional | Extracts the direct telephone string of the commercial issuer for administrative communication matrices.18 |
| /Invoice/cac:AccountingSupplierParty/cac:Party/cac:Contact/cbc:ElectronicMail | supplier.contact.email | String | Optional | Extracts the primary digital communication address for the commercial issuer.18 |
| /Invoice/cac:AccountingCustomerParty/cac:Party/cac:PartyIdentification/cbc:ID | customer.vkn | String | Conditional | Extracts the 10-digit corporate identifier for the purchasing entity (Alıcı). Required to verify transaction volumes against the LOB (Borçlu İşlem Hacmi) limits.1 |
| /Invoice/cac:AccountingCustomerParty/cac:Party/cac:PartyName/cbc:Name | customer.name | String | Required | Captures the legal corporate title of the buyer, crucial for Murabaha proxy (vekalet) verification workflows.1 |
| /Invoice/cac:Delivery/cac:DeliveryLocation/cac:Address/cbc:StreetName | delivery.street | String | Optional | Defines the primary physical vector for goods delivery.13 |
| /Invoice/cac:Delivery/cac:DeliveryLocation/cac:Address/cbc:CityName | delivery.city | String | Optional | Indicates the geographic terminus for the commercial shipment, utilized to verify logical logistics routes against Konşimento documents.13 |
| /Invoice/cac:Delivery/cac:DeliveryLocation/cac:Address/cac:AddressLine/cbc:Line | delivery.address\_line | String | Optional | Unstructured, supplementary geographical routing instructions.20 |
| /Invoice/cac:PaymentMeans/cbc:PaymentMeansCode | payment.means\_code | String | Optional | Defines the expected settlement mechanism extracted from the UNCL 4461 code list (e.g., code 30 representing Credit Transfer, or code 58 representing SEPA).7 |
| /Invoice/cac:PaymentMeans/cbc:PaymentID | payment.payment\_id | String | Optional | Captures unstructured remittance information or internal settlement tracking identifiers established between commercial partners.7 |
| /Invoice/cac:PaymentMeans/cac:PayeeFinancialAccount/cbc:ID | payment.iban | String | Optional | The paramount financial routing parameter. Extracts the International Bank Account Number representing the destination for the preliminary disbursement (ön ödeme).1 |
| /Invoice/cac:PaymentMeans/cac:PayeeFinancialAccount/cac:FinancialInstitutionBranch/cbc:ID | payment.bic | String | Optional | Extracts the Business Identifier Code (BIC) accompanying international or highly structured domestic IBAN routing.7 |
| /Invoice/cac:PaymentTerms/cbc:Note | payment.terms\_note | String | Optional | A purely unstructured textual description of specific payment conditions, such as early settlement discounts or delayed maturity cycles (e.g., "Net within 30 days").28 |
| /Invoice/cac:DespatchDocumentReference/cbc:ID | references.despatch\_id | Array | Optional | Extracts the exact 16-character string bridging the financial invoice definitively to its preceding physical movement record (e-İrsaliye).1 |
| /Invoice/cac:DespatchDocumentReference/cbc:IssueDate | references.despatch\_date | Array | Optional | The temporal anchor of physical transit. The canonical schema evaluates this string to ensure goods were physically documented prior to the issuance of the financial demand.1 |
| /Invoice/cac:TaxTotal/cbc:TaxAmount | tax.total\_tax\_amount | Float | Required | Extracts the aggregate sum of all assessed taxes across all distinct tax schemes, expressed uniformly in the document's accounting currency.26 |
| /Invoice/cac:TaxTotal/cac:TaxSubtotal/cbc:TaxableAmount | tax.subtotals.taxable\_base | Float | Required | Defines the foundational monetary amount subject to a highly specific, tiered VAT or Tevkifat category.32 |
| /Invoice/cac:TaxTotal/cac:TaxSubtotal/cbc:TaxAmount | tax.subtotals.tax\_amount | Float | Required | Defines the resulting specific tax extraction for that sub-tier, rounded strictly to a maximum of two decimal places.30 |
| /Invoice/cac:TaxTotal/cac:TaxSubtotal/cac:TaxCategory/cbc:Percent | tax.subtotals.percent | Float | Required | The discrete mathematical percentage multiplier assigned to the specific tax tier.12 |
| /Invoice/cac:TaxTotal/cac:TaxSubtotal/cac:TaxCategory/cac:TaxScheme/cbc:ID | tax.subtotals.scheme\_id | String | Required | The regulatory classification code determining the nature of the tax operation. Evaluates standard VAT (KDV Code 0015), Special Consumption Tax (ÖTV Code 0071), or Withholding arrays (Tevkifat Codes 601-625).36 |
| /Invoice/cac:LegalMonetaryTotal/cbc:LineExtensionAmount | totals.line\_extension | Float | Required | The cumulative arithmetic aggregation of every individual line item's net amount, prior to the application of overarching document-level taxes and discounts.12 |
| /Invoice/cac:LegalMonetaryTotal/cbc:TaxExclusiveAmount | totals.tax\_exclusive | Float | Required | The total valuation of the commercial invoice explicitly excluding the total applied tax array.12 |
| /Invoice/cac:LegalMonetaryTotal/cbc:TaxInclusiveAmount | totals.tax\_inclusive | Float | Required | The total valuation of the commercial invoice definitively including the total applied tax array.12 |
| /Invoice/cac:LegalMonetaryTotal/cbc:AllowanceTotalAmount | totals.allowance\_total | Float | Optional | The aggregate sum of all document-level financial deductions, conditional rebates, and commercial discounts.12 |
| /Invoice/cac:LegalMonetaryTotal/cbc:ChargeTotalAmount | totals.charge\_total | Float | Optional | The aggregate sum of all document-level financial additions, non-tax fees, or handling costs.12 |
| /Invoice/cac:LegalMonetaryTotal/cbc:PayableAmount | totals.payable\_amount | Float | Required | The absolute, final mathematical demand constituting the core of the factoring assignment. Must align flawlessly with the institutional Borçlu İşlem Hacmi limit.1 |
| /Invoice/cac:InvoiceLine/cbc:ID | lines.line\_id | String | Required | The sequential alphanumeric identifier denoting a specific, granular commercial transaction component.12 |
| /Invoice/cac:InvoiceLine/cbc:InvoicedQuantity | lines.quantity | Float | Required | The explicit volumetric measurement, dimensional extent, or numerical count of the transacted commercial assets.12 |
| /Invoice/cac:InvoiceLine/cbc:InvoicedQuantity/@unitCode | lines.unit\_code | String | Required | The standardized unit classifier string originating from the UN/ECE Recommendation No. 20 codelist (e.g., C62 for unitary pieces, KGM for kilograms).1 |
| /Invoice/cac:InvoiceLine/cbc:LineExtensionAmount | lines.net\_amount | Float | Required | The discrete mathematical valuation of the specific line item, explicitly devoid of VAT but incorporating any underlying line-level discount logic.12 |
| /Invoice/cac:InvoiceLine/cac:AllowanceCharge/cbc:ChargeIndicator | lines.allowance.is\_charge | Boolean | Optional | The critical boolean toggle determining the arithmetic function. A "false" value commands a deduction (allowance), whereas a "true" value dictates an addition (charge).12 |
| /Invoice/cac:InvoiceLine/cac:AllowanceCharge/cbc:AllowanceChargeReasonCode | lines.allowance.reason\_code | String | Optional | Extracts the codified rationale for the financial modification, mapping to UNCL 5189 for allowances and UNCL 7161 for charges.48 |
| /Invoice/cac:InvoiceLine/cac:AllowanceCharge/cbc:Amount | lines.allowance.amount | Float | Optional | The absolute, isolated monetary value of the line-level modifier, bounded to a maximum of two decimal places.12 |
| /Invoice/cac:InvoiceLine/cac:AllowanceCharge/cbc:MultiplierFactorNumeric | lines.allowance.multiplier | Float | Optional | The explicit percentage parameter utilized mathematically alongside the base amount to deduce the final allowance or charge.48 |
| /Invoice/cac:InvoiceLine/cac:Item/cbc:Name | lines.item\_name | String | Required | The natural language, semantic description defining the exact commercial good. This node is an absolute requirement for Shariah compliance evaluations, empowering specialized LLMs to compute semantic equivalence against unstructured Murabaha contracts.1 |
| /Invoice/cac:InvoiceLine/cac:Item/cac:ClassifiedTaxCategory/cbc:Percent | lines.tax\_percent | Float | Optional | Specifies the highly granular tax rate applied exclusively to this discrete line item, independent of the overarching document calculations.12 |

## **4\. The Canonical JSON Schema Architecture**

The fundamental philosophy driving the canonical schema design is structural normalization and relational integrity.8 By stripping away the bloated XML envelope mechanics, namespaces, and deeply nested repetition, the parser generates an elegant, computationally inexpensive JSON object.3 This schema operates as the definitive, single source of truth for all subsequent institutional consistency algorithms.  
The schema is rigorously constructed in adherence to the JSON Schema Draft 7 standard, ensuring absolute type safety, enforced pattern matching (via Regular Expressions), and mandatory field compliance. This long, highly structured schema dictates the exact boundaries of a valid e-Fatura data representation within the Turkish financial ecosystem.

JSON

{  
  "$schema": "http://json-schema.org/draft-07/schema\#",  
  "title": "Canonical\_UBL\_TR\_1\_2",  
  "description": "Exhaustive normalization schema for Turkish UBL-TR 1.2 e-Fatura integration",  
  "type": "object",  
  "properties": {  
    "metadata": {  
      "type": "object",  
      "properties": {  
        "ubl\_version": { "type": "string", "const": "2.1" },  
        "customization\_id": { "type": "string", "pattern": "^TR1\\\\.2$" },  
        "profile\_id": { "type": "string", "enum": }  
      },  
      "required": \["ubl\_version", "customization\_id", "profile\_id"\]  
    },  
    "document": {  
      "type": "object",  
      "properties": {  
        "invoice\_id": { "type": "string", "pattern": "^\[A-Z0-9\]{3}20\[2-9\]\[0-9\]\[0-9\]{9}$" },  
        "uuid": { "type": "string", "format": "uuid" },  
        "issue\_date": { "type": "string", "format": "date-time" },  
        "type\_code": { "type": "string" },  
        "currency\_code": { "type": "string", "pattern": "^\[A-Z\]{3}$" },  
        "period\_start": { "type": "string", "format": "date" },  
        "period\_end": { "type": "string", "format": "date" }  
      },  
      "required": \["invoice\_id", "uuid", "issue\_date", "type\_code", "currency\_code"\]  
    },  
    "supplier": {  
      "type": "object",  
      "properties": {  
        "vkn": { "type": "string", "pattern": "^\\\\d{10}$" },  
        "tckn": { "type": "string", "pattern": "^\\\\d{11}$" },  
        "name": { "type": "string", "minLength": 2 },  
        "contact": {  
          "type": "object",  
          "properties": {  
            "phone": { "type": "string" },  
            "email": { "type": "string", "format": "email" }  
          }  
        }  
      },  
      "anyOf": \[  
        { "required": \["vkn", "name"\] },  
        { "required": \["tckn", "name"\] }  
      \]  
    },  
    "customer": {  
      "type": "object",  
      "properties": {  
        "vkn": { "type": "string", "pattern": "^\\\\d{10}$" },  
        "tckn": { "type": "string", "pattern": "^\\\\d{11}$" },  
        "name": { "type": "string", "minLength": 2 }  
      },  
      "anyOf": \[  
        { "required": \["vkn", "name"\] },  
        { "required": \["tckn", "name"\] }  
      \]  
    },  
    "delivery": {  
      "type": "object",  
      "properties": {  
        "street": { "type": "string" },  
        "city": { "type": "string" },  
        "address\_line": { "type": "string" }  
      }  
    },  
    "references": {  
      "type": "object",  
      "properties": {  
        "despatch\_advices": {  
          "type": "array",  
          "items": {  
            "type": "object",  
            "properties": {  
              "despatch\_id": { "type": "string", "pattern": "^\[A-Z0-9\]{16}$" },  
              "despatch\_date": { "type": "string", "format": "date" }  
            },  
            "required": \["despatch\_id"\]  
          }  
        }  
      }  
    },  
    "payment": {  
      "type": "object",  
      "properties": {  
        "means\_code": { "type": "string", "pattern": "^\\\\d{1,2}$" },  
        "payment\_id": { "type": "string" },  
        "iban": { "type": "string", "pattern": "^\[A-Z\]{2}\\\\d{2}\[A-Z0-9\]{11,30}$" },  
        "bic": { "type": "string", "pattern": "^\[A-Z\]{6}\[A-Z2-9\]\[A-NP-Z0-9\](\[A-Z0-9\]{3})?$" },  
        "terms\_note": { "type": "string" }  
      }  
    },  
    "tax": {  
      "type": "object",  
      "properties": {  
        "total\_tax\_amount": { "type": "number", "multipleOf": 0.01 },  
        "subtotals": {  
          "type": "array",  
          "items": {  
            "type": "object",  
            "properties": {  
              "taxable\_base": { "type": "number", "multipleOf": 0.01 },  
              "tax\_amount": { "type": "number", "multipleOf": 0.01 },  
              "percent": { "type": "number" },  
              "scheme\_id": { "type": "string", "pattern": "^\\\\d{4}$" }  
            },  
            "required": \["taxable\_base", "tax\_amount", "percent", "scheme\_id"\]  
          },  
          "minItems": 1  
        }  
      },  
      "required": \["total\_tax\_amount"\]  
    },  
    "totals": {  
      "type": "object",  
      "properties": {  
        "line\_extension": { "type": "number", "multipleOf": 0.01 },  
        "tax\_exclusive": { "type": "number", "multipleOf": 0.01 },  
        "tax\_inclusive": { "type": "number", "multipleOf": 0.01 },  
        "allowance\_total": { "type": "number", "multipleOf": 0.01 },  
        "charge\_total": { "type": "number", "multipleOf": 0.01 },  
        "payable\_amount": { "type": "number", "multipleOf": 0.01 }  
      },  
      "required": \["line\_extension", "tax\_exclusive", "tax\_inclusive", "payable\_amount"\]  
    },  
    "lines": {  
      "type": "array",  
      "items": {  
        "type": "object",  
        "properties": {  
          "line\_id": { "type": "string" },  
          "item\_name": { "type": "string", "minLength": 1 },  
          "quantity": { "type": "number" },  
          "unit\_code": { "type": "string", "minLength": 2, "maxLength": 3 },  
          "net\_amount": { "type": "number", "multipleOf": 0.01 },  
          "tax\_percent": { "type": "number" },  
          "allowances\_charges": {  
            "type": "array",  
            "items": {  
              "type": "object",  
              "properties": {  
                "is\_charge": { "type": "boolean" },  
                "amount": { "type": "number", "multipleOf": 0.01 },  
                "reason\_code": { "type": "string" },  
                "multiplier": { "type": "number" }  
              },  
              "required": \["is\_charge", "amount"\]  
            }  
          }  
        },  
        "required": \["line\_id", "item\_name", "quantity", "unit\_code", "net\_amount"\]  
      },  
      "minItems": 1  
    }  
  },  
  "required": \["metadata", "document", "supplier", "customer", "tax", "totals", "lines"\],  
  "additionalProperties": false  
}

This architecture explicitly forbids the injection of undefined properties through the "additionalProperties": false constraint, establishing an airtight parameter space. By enforcing numerical precision bounds ("multipleOf": 0.01) and strict identity string patterns, the JSON object ensures that the data delivered to the mathematical engine is pre-validated for formatting consistency, isolating the downstream logic strictly to commercial validation.

## **5\. Deterministic Normalization Rules and Transformation Logic**

The transition from a raw UBL-TR XML array to the structured canonical JSON is governed by a set of uncompromising normalization directives. Direct extraction via XPath without programmatic remediation inevitably results in mathematical inconsistencies and type-matching failures during downstream execution. The parsing engine executes the following transformations in real-time.  
**Rule Alpha: Temporal Data Synthesis and ISO-8601 Formatting** The UBL-TR standard frequently compartmentalizes chronological data into distinct date and time components (e.g., \<cbc:IssueDate\> and \<cbc:IssueTime\>).1 To execute exact chronological sequence validations against physical transport documents, the parsing engine must synthesize these disparate strings into a unified, mathematically comparable ISO-8601 UTC timestamp format.

* *XML Input Configuration:* \<cbc:IssueDate\>2026-11-20\</cbc:IssueDate\> accompanied by \<cbc:IssueTime\>15:45:30\</cbc:IssueTime\>  
* *Deterministic Logic:* The engine verifies the existence of both nodes, executing a concatenation operation separated by the literal character "T", appending a "Z" to denote coordinated universal time.  
* *Canonical JSON Output:* "issue\_date": "2026-11-20T15:45:30Z"

**Rule Beta: Numeric Type Casting and Arbitrary Precision Rounding** Within the XML envelope, floating-point monetary values are represented purely as character strings enclosed by nodes such as \<cbc:LineExtensionAmount currencyID="TRY"\>1436.5\</cbc:LineExtensionAmount\>.12 During normalization, these strings must be rigorously cast into absolute numeric types. To prevent microscopic floating-point discrepancies across diverse coding environments, the parser implements half-up arithmetic rounding, explicitly constraining all monetary variables to a maximum of two decimal places, which aligns with fundamental accounting principles.17

* *XML Input Configuration:* \<cbc:TaxableAmount\>1945.004\</cbc:TaxableAmount\>  
* *Deterministic Logic:* Math.round(parseFloat(node.text) \* 100\) / 100  
* *Canonical JSON Output:* "taxable\_base": 1945.00

**Rule Gamma: Boolean Resolution of Financial Modifiers** Within the cac:AllowanceCharge architecture, the cbc:ChargeIndicator acts as the definitive mathematical switch.48 A "false" statement designates a commercial discount resulting in a monetary deduction, whereas a "true" statement defines an addition, such as a handling fee or penalty.34 The raw XML often presents these switches with irregular casing (False, FALSE, true).12 The normalizer executes a strict lowercase string comparison to cast the value into a native JSON boolean.

* *XML Input Configuration:* \<cbc:ChargeIndicator\>False\</cbc:ChargeIndicator\>  
* *Deterministic Logic:* node.text.trim().toLowerCase() \=== "true"  
* *Canonical JSON Output:* "is\_charge": false

**Rule Delta: Codified Rationale Extraction (Tax and Payment Schemes)** Turkish commercial documentation leverages highly granular, institutionally specific codes rather than descriptive natural language. The engine must extract these codes without modification. For instance, payment instructions map to the UNCL 4461 list where "30" represents a domestic credit transfer and "58" specifies an international SEPA routing.7 Similarly, the tax architecture differentiates standard KDV (0015) from specialized Tevkifat mechanisms (601-625).36

* *XML Input Configuration:* \<cac:TaxScheme\>\<cbc:ID schemeID="UN/ECE 5153"\>0015\</cbc:ID\>\</cac:TaxScheme\>  
* *Deterministic Logic:* Direct string extraction.  
* *Canonical JSON Output:* "scheme\_id": "0015"

**Rule Epsilon: Volumetric Measurement Standardization** Commercial line items define physical quantities using the UN/ECE Recommendation No. 20 codelist embedded within the unitCode parameter.1 The parser evaluates this attribute string (e.g., C62 for individual pieces, KGM for kilograms, MTR for meters) and assigns it directly to the canonical structure to facilitate future algorithmic comparisons against maritime Bills of Lading (Konşimento) or physical despatch advices.1

* *XML Input Configuration:* \<cbc:InvoicedQuantity unitCode="KGM"\>250.5\</cbc:InvoicedQuantity\>  
* *Deterministic Logic:* Attribute extraction combined with numerical casting.  
* *Canonical JSON Output:* "quantity": 250.5, "unit\_code": "KGM"

## **6\. Mathematical Verification Protocols and Tutar Tutarlılığı**

Once the raw XML is securely transformed into the structured canonical JSON schema, the system executes a rigorous battery of deterministic mathematical formulas. The principle of tutar tutarlılığı (amount consistency) demands that a properly constructed commercial document acts as a mathematically sealed ecosystem.1 Any internal discrepancy indicates a profound breakdown in the accounting logic or represents a manual alteration of the document that invalidates its utility within a trade finance workflow.

### **The Mathematics of Subtotal Aggregation**

The programmatic recalculation of the invoice operates through a cascading verification tree, aggregating from the granular item level up to the total financial demand.1  
The primary evaluation commences at the line item level, where the system computes the discrete net valuation:  
![][image1]  
Each individual result is cross-referenced against the stated lines.net\_amount. Subsequently, the system calculates the overarching document sum:  
![][image2]  
This mathematical aggregate is instantly compared against the explicitly defined totals.tax\_exclusive field.12  
Finally, the verification sequence incorporates the extensive taxation arrays. The engine navigates the tax.subtotals block, iterating over every distinct KDV or Tevkifat category:  
![][image3]  
Any discrepancy—even a fractional variance of a single cent—between the programmatic result and the stated totals.payable\_amount generates an immediate inconsistency record, indicating an absolute failure of the commercial math.1

### **Modulus-10 Algorithmic Verification for Corporate Identity (Parti Kimliği)**

The structural integrity of corporate identity within the Turkish market relies entirely upon the mathematics of the Vergi Kimlik Numarası (VKN) and the Türkiye Cumhuriyeti Kimlik Numarası (TCKN).1 The extraction of a TCKN from the cac:AccountingSupplierParty reveals an 11-digit numeric sequence where the final two digits serve as cryptographic check sums derived through a precise Modulus-10 sequence applied to the preceding nine digits.55  
The verification engine executes the following arithmetic on the extracted string to confirm validity:  
Let ![][image4] represent the individual numeric components.  
![][image5]  
The resultant value must flawlessly equal ![][image6].55 Subsequently, a secondary evaluation computes the final digit:  
![][image7]  
The resultant integer must identically match ![][image8].55 If the evaluated supplier.tckn string fails this intrinsic modulus calculation, the document exhibits a severe structural discrepancy, confirming that the stated corporate identity is a statistical impossibility and an artificial fabrication.1

### **Modulus-97 Algorithmic Verification for Financial Disbursement**

The payment.iban extracted from the cac:PayeeFinancialAccount/cbc:ID array requires uncompromising validation to ensure secure capital transfer during the factoring alacak devri preliminary disbursement (ön ödeme) phase.1 Turkish IBAN structures strictly adhere to the international ISO 13616 standard, presenting a 26-character alphanumeric identifier.57  
The integrity of this financial routing parameter is verified through a Modulus-97 operation defined by ISO/IEC 7064\.59 The programmatic sequence shifts the initial four characters (the Country Code "TR" and the subsequent two Check Digits) to the end of the identifier string.59 The engine translates all alphabetical characters into a dual-digit numeric representation (![][image9]), interpreting the entire reconstructed sequence as a massive integer.59  
![][image10]  
An operational IBAN is confirmed definitively and exclusively when the resultant mathematical remainder is exactly 1\.57 Failure to yield a remainder of 1 denotes an invalid account structure, presenting an inconsistency that requires immediate remediation prior to any financial execution.

## **7\. Edge Case Catalog and Structural Remediation**

The practical exchange of commercial data within the GİB ecosystem frequently produces anomalous encoding patterns and structural variations that challenge standard linear parsers. The canonical normalizer integrates specialized remediation protocols designed to address highly specific, recurring edge cases that cause unrefined XML traversal functions to fail catastrophically. The table below catalogs these documented structural variations and the exact programmatic strategy employed to maintain analytical continuity.

| Structural Edge Case | Manifestation in UBL-TR XML | Canonical Normalization Strategy | Validation Outcome / Implication |
| :---- | :---- | :---- | :---- |
| **Orphaned Financial Allowances** | The document declares a monetary discount within the cac:LegalMonetaryTotal/cbc:AllowanceTotalAmount aggregate but omits the required descriptive arrays within the overarching cac:AllowanceCharge node.34 | Extract the total allowance mathematically to maintain arithmetic balance, automatically inserting a synthetic "reason": "UNDEFINED\_DOCUMENT\_ALLOWANCE" into the canonical payload. | The system establishes mathematical consistency but generates a format discrepancy record due to the violation of GİB requirements for contextual charge justification.50 |
| **Cascading Tevkifat Modifiers** | The cac:TaxTotal array exhibits multiple nested standard VAT (KDV) and Withholding (Tevkifat) sub-schemes (e.g., Code 601, Code 604\) applied simultaneously against an identical TaxableAmount.37 | Flatten the deeply nested taxation arrays into distinct, independent JSON objects within the tax.subtotals array, isolating the exact scheme\_id codes.31 | The programmatic recalculation engine executes the KDV addition sequence first, subsequently subtracting the Tevkifat allocations to determine the precise final payable.40 |
| **Unit Dimension Mismatches** | The line item volumetric measure within cac:InvoiceLine/cbc:InvoicedQuantity specifies an obscure or customized unit identifier (e.g., "27" indicating theoretical ton) instead of a recognized standard UN/ECE Rec 20 metric.45 | Bypass codelist rejection and preserve the non-standard alphanumeric identifier string exactly within the lines.unit\_code canonical field.45 | Prompts semantic NLP algorithms to evaluate the identifier contextually against the physical gross weights listed on the Konşimento transport document.1 |
| **Omission of Payment Mechanisms** | The XML envelope entirely lacks the cac:PaymentMeans and associated cac:PayeeFinancialAccount nodes, completely obscuring explicit disbursement paths and IBAN data.7 | Nullify the payment object entirely within the canonical JSON payload, maintaining schema validity without inserting placeholder data. | The system generates a severe consistency check failure regarding capital routing, mandating manual verification of the IBAN exclusively via the OCR analysis of the physical AB-NTR assignment form.1 |
| **Contradictory Currency Parameters** | The document's overarching cbc:DocumentCurrencyCode (e.g., USD) contradicts the inline currencyID properties located on individual cbc:LineExtensionAmount or cbc:TaxAmount properties (e.g., TRY).16 | Extract all inline currency attributes and construct an explicit discrepancy report array mapped directly to the canonical output.17 | Yields an absolute mathematical invalidation; calculating aggregate totals across divergent currencies represents a fundamental formatting inconsistency requiring commercial re-issuance. |
| **ZATCA-style Prepayment Compression** | A singular cac:InvoiceLine contains a massive array of distinct cac:Item objects representing disparate goods bundled under a single prepayment line, violating standard one-to-one UBL cardinality.35 | The extraction algorithm restricts data capture strictly to the primary cac:Item/cbc:Name encountered within the DOM array, disregarding subsequent nested items to maintain relational database integrity.62 | Yields a structural format inconsistency marker, highlighting the improper bundling of commercial goods within a single financial ledger line.62 |

## **8\. Exhaustive Parse-Time Validation Checklist**

To safeguard the operational ecosystem of Turkish trade finance, the execution from raw XML into the normalized canonical JSON schema operates as the primary gateway for immediate structural and logical validation.1 The extraction engine runs an unyielding sequence of checks prior to committing the data to the evidence database, verifying that the document strictly conforms to commercial reality.  
The parse-time validation workflow executes the following rigid sequence:

1. **Foundational Envelope and Syntax Verification:** The parser traverses the UBL header, explicitly confirming the presence of the string "2.1" within the cbc:UBLVersionID node and "TR1.2" within the cbc:CustomizationID node.2 Any alteration, omission, or deviation immediately invalidates the payload, indicating the document was generated outside the authorized Özel Entegratör ecosystem.1  
2. **Cryptographic Hash Isolation:** The unique signature hash residing within the cbc:UUID node is isolated and verified for proper format alignment. This UUID serves as the irrefutable digital fingerprint of the document, utilized subsequently to identify if an identical invoice is presented multiple times within the same operational workflow, neutralizing internal duplication inconsistencies.1  
3. **Identity Equivalence Confirmation:** The verification algorithm examines the 10 or 11 numeric characters isolated within the cac:AccountingSupplierParty/cac:PartyIdentification/cbc:ID array.1 This specific value undergoes the aforementioned Modulus-10 arithmetic validation and is explicitly mapped for cross-referencing against the Optical Character Recognition (OCR) data extracted from the physical Tüzel Kişi Müşteri Bilgi Formu.1  
4. **Temporal Chronological Sequencing:** A mathematical string comparison evaluates the normalized ISO-8601 string within document.issue\_date against the temporal markers located within the references.despatch\_date arrays.1 This confirms the unbending law of commercial physics: the physical preparation and despatch of goods (documented by the e-İrsaliye) must logically and temporally precede the issuance of the financial demand.1  
5. **Absolute Monetary Recalculation:** The massive array of extracted lines.net\_amount values is systematically aggregated and mathematically verified against the overarching totals.line\_extension.12 Subsequently, document-level modifiers and intricate taxation cascades (tax.total\_tax\_amount) are programmatically applied to verify exact equivalence with the final totals.payable\_amount parameter, securing total tutar tutarlılığı.1  
6. **Semantic Commodity Validation:** The engine inspects every lines.item\_name entry to guarantee the presence of a valid, meaningful natural language string.1 This ensures the canonical payload possesses the necessary semantic density required by downstream Large Language Models (LLMs) to execute compliance evaluations against unstructured Murabaha contracts or international Bills of Lading.1  
7. **Financial Routing Integrity:** The payment.iban undergoes strict structural assessment and Modulus-97 verification.1 The validated IBAN is then queued for direct comparison against the manual payout instructions detailed on the Alacak Bildirim Formu (AB-NTR), closing the final operational loop and ensuring secure capital deployment.1

By enforcing this exhaustive technical normalization and verification sequence, financial institutions operating within the Turkish market eradicate the administrative friction generated by disparate document formats. The canonical JSON schema transforms complex, subjective XML interpretations into an immutable foundation of mathematical and logical certainty, securing the integrity of the entire commercial finance operation.

#### **Alıntılanan çalışmalar**

1. Trade Finance Document Pack Report

Units of Measure \[UNECE Recommendation No. 20\] \- MITI, erişim tarihi Şubat 19, 2026, [https://www.miti.gov.my/miti/resources/Preferential%20Certificate%20of%20Origin/ATIGA%20e-Form%20D/Appendix\_9\_Updated\_MIG\_0\_15.3\_.pdf](https://www.miti.gov.my/miti/resources/Preferential%20Certificate%20of%20Origin/ATIGA%20e-Form%20D/Appendix_9_Updated_MIG_0_15.3_.pdf)
